title: Dŵr Cymru: Monitoring Alert Issued for Wye and Usk Catchments
locationName: Powys | Builth Wells | Brecon and Radnorshire
evidence: Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water issues an internal monitoring alert for key water infrastructure in the Wye and Usk catchments due to forecasted heavy rainfall. No disruption reported. Routine precautionary monitoring elevated. Field teams placed on readiness.

assessment.justification: Precautionary monitoring alert only. No infrastructure disruption confirmed. Standard adverse weather protocol for catchment-sensitive water infrastructure. Early-phase upstream signal.
assessment.synthesis: Dŵr Cymru precautionary monitoring alert for Wye and Usk catchments. No disruption. Standard protocol. Background infrastructure signal.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Precautionary monitoring only. No disruption confirmed.

title: Dŵr Cymru: Wastewater Network Stress – Abergavenny Area
evidence: Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water reports significant stress on the wastewater network in the Abergavenny area due to surface water infiltration. Risk of combined sewer overflow into the River Usk. Operational teams monitoring and responding. No supply disruption at this time.

assessment.justification: Official water company report of wastewater network stress in Abergavenny. Combined sewer overflow risk adds environmental dimension to the flooding picture. Consistent with rainfall volumes and the scale of flooding in this area.
assessment.synthesis: Wastewater network under stress in Abergavenny with combined sewer overflow risk to the River Usk. Adds environmental impact dimension to the SE Wales flooding picture.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Official water company report. Environmental impact risk consistent with flooding scale.

title: Dŵr Cymru: Newport Wastewater Pumping Station at Risk of Flooding
evidence: Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water reports that a key wastewater pumping station in Newport is at risk of flood inundation. Temporary pumping equipment being deployed. If station floods, risk of sewage discharge to the River Usk. Emergency plan activated.

assessment.justification: Official confirmation of critical wastewater infrastructure at risk in Newport. Emergency plan activation indicates severity. Sewage discharge risk to the Usk adds significant environmental impact if not mitigated.
assessment.synthesis: Critical wastewater pumping station in Newport at risk of flooding. Emergency measures deployed. Sewage discharge risk to the River Usk adds an environmental emergency dimension to the event.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Critical infrastructure at risk confirmed. Emergency plan activated.

title: Dŵr Cymru: Water Supply Interruption – Pontypool and Cwmbran
evidence: Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water confirms water supply interruption affecting approximately 1,500 properties in parts of Pontypool and Cwmbran following damage to a water main during flooding. Bottled water distribution point being established. Repair teams deployed but access restricted.

assessment.justification: Official water supply interruption confirmed for 1,500 properties in Torfaen. Bottled water distribution adds community welfare dimension. Access restrictions for repair teams mirror road flooding conditions in the area.
assessment.synthesis: Water supply interrupted for 1,500 properties in Pontypool and Cwmbran following main damage. Bottled water distribution established. Adds a critical community welfare dimension to the ongoing SE Wales event.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Official supply interruption confirmed. Community welfare impact significant.

title: Severn Trent: River Wye Monitoring – Abstraction Suspended near Ross-on-Wye
evidence: Severn Trent Water suspends abstraction at the River Wye intake near Ross-on-Wye due to high turbidity and elevated river levels. River at 4.2m and rising, making water treatment impractical. Backup reservoir supplies activated. No supply disruption at present.

assessment.justification: Severn Trent official action at Ross-on-Wye abstraction point is consistent with EA gauge data showing 4.2m and rising. Turbidity-driven abstraction suspension is a credible operational response. Adds infrastructure dimension to Herefordshire picture.
assessment.synthesis: Wye abstraction suspended near Ross-on-Wye due to high turbidity and river levels. Consistent with EA gauge data. Adds water infrastructure impact to the Herefordshire picture.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Official abstraction suspension confirmed. Consistent with river gauge data.

title: Dŵr Cymru: Combined Sewer Overflows Reported – Multiple SE Wales Locations
evidence: Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water reports combined sewer overflows occurring at multiple locations across Southeast Wales including Cwmbran, Pontypool, and parts of Caerphilly. Network capacity exceeded by surface water infiltration. Environmental impact being assessed. Regulatory notification issued to Natural Resources Wales.

assessment.justification: Official combined sewer overflow report covering multiple SE Wales locations. Regulatory notification to NRW indicates the company is managing a significant, multi-site environmental event. Consistent with the scale of rainfall and surface water accumulation.
assessment.synthesis: Combined sewer overflows confirmed at multiple SE Wales locations. Regulatory notification issued. Adds environmental infrastructure failure dimension to the SE Wales event picture.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Multi-site official report with regulatory notification. Environmental impact confirmed.

title: Bristol Water: Turbidity Monitoring Elevated – Somerset Levels Catchment
evidence: Bristol Water reports elevated turbidity at the Cheddar and Chew Valley reservoir intakes due to surface run-off from the Somerset plateau. Water treatment processes adjusted. No supply disruption. Increased chemical dosing applied as a precautionary measure.

assessment.justification: Bristol Water turbidity alert at Somerset intakes is consistent with heavy rainfall running off the Mendip Hills and Somerset plateau. Standard operational response with no supply disruption. Adds water infrastructure dimension to the Somerset picture.
assessment.synthesis: Elevated turbidity at Bristol Water Somerset intakes. Precautionary treatment adjustment in place. No supply disruption. Adds infrastructure monitoring signal from the Somerset catchment.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Official turbidity monitoring alert. No disruption. Standard operational response.

title: Thames Water: River Kennet Monitoring – Abstraction Under Review Near Newbury
evidence: Thames Water places River Kennet abstraction near Newbury under precautionary review as levels rise and turbidity increases. No abstraction suspension at this time. Backup groundwater supplies confirmed available. Monitoring will continue through the afternoon.

assessment.justification: Thames Water precautionary monitoring of the River Kennet near Newbury is consistent with rainfall accumulation in the upper Kennet catchment. No disruption at present. Adds water infrastructure signal from the Thames Valley area.
assessment.synthesis: Thames Water precautionary monitoring of Kennet abstraction near Newbury. No supply disruption. Consistent with rainfall in the upper Thames catchment. Adds a central England water infrastructure dimension.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Precautionary monitoring only. No disruption confirmed.
